channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Say hello to HaLow: Wi-Fi routers that can send 250Mbps across 10 miles (yes, 10 miles) have been demoed at CES 2025 and I'm very excited
item.description: Wi-Fi HaLow boasts 9.9-mile range and sub-GHz waves promising reliable, long-distance connectivity for IoT and industrial setups.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: The TikTok ban is being weighed by the Supreme Court. Here’s what to know
item.description: The Supreme Court of the United States is hearing arguments today to decide the fate of TikTok. Lawmakers believe the app, which is owned by the Chinese company ByteDance, poses a threat to national security.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Happy New Year! We begin the new year with the top five procurement themes that will shape the federal acquisition marketplace in 2025 and beyond
item.description: 2025 will see continued focus on supply chain resiliency, often revolving around support for and investment in the domestic industrial base.
item.dc:creator: Roger Waldron, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta puts the ‘Dead Internet Theory’ into practice
item.description: Meta’s mission statement is to “build the future of human connection and the technology that makes it possible.” According to Meta, the future of human connection is basically humans connecting with AI.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Enterprises willing to spend up to $250 million on gen AI, but ROI remains elusive
item.description: A sharp rise in enterprise investments in generative AI is poised to reshape business operations, with 68% of companies planning to invest between $50 million and $250 million over the next year, according to KPMG’s latest AI Quarterly Pulse Survey.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The 10 most in-demand tech jobs for 2025
item.description: As we head into 2025, businesses are dialed in on hiring roles such as software engineer, software developer, and end-user support, as well roles centered around cloud, database management, and systems engineering and administration, according to Robert Half Technology’s 2025 IT salary report.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Easy Photo Printing: A Guide for Android and iPhone Users
item.description: Discover the simplicity of printing high-quality photos from your smartphone using wireless printers, apps, or online services.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: From AI assistants to holographic displays, automakers showcase in-cabin experiences at CES
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Interior lighting aimed at reducing motion sickness. Shiny holographic dashboards displayed on windshields. And AI-powered voice assistants paired with infotainment systems to keep you company on long drives.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AI at CES: a look at the wild PCs, smart glasses, and body-scanning mirrors turning heads at the conference
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Akeneo aims to transform the retail playbook with AI and data consistency
item.description: Australian retailers have spent much of the last few years buffeted by economic challenges. In 2024, squeezed by the rising cost of living, inflationary impact, and interest rates, they are now grappling with declining consumer spending and confidence.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: What to know as TikTok argues in front of Supreme Court
item.description: TikTok is hoping to invalidate a law that would force it to divest from its Chinese parent company or face a ban.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Unlocking business growth: How CIOs can blend strategy and innovation to drive success
item.description: The role of the CIO has evolved. Today’s CIOs are expected to do more than manage IT infrastructure and operations. A savvy CIO must go beyond traditional expectations, driving innovation and aligning technology with business strategy to deliver measurable business value.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Meta tries letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings as way to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Meta rolls back hate speech rules as Zuckerberg cites 'recent elections' as a catalyst
item.description: It wasn't just fact-checking that Meta scrapped from its platforms as it prepares for the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Closing the digital gap: The importance of secure development practices in government IT modernization
item.description: In today's fast-paced digital landscape, effective government IT modernization is crucial for agencies to achieve their missions. The post Closing the digital gap: The importance of secure development practices in government IT modernization first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Building generative AI applications is too hard, developers say
item.description: When everyone touts the benefits of generative artificial intelligence, telling the world how much easier it will make all of our lives, many developers who actually have to build those miracle apps are of a different opinion, according to a new survey sponsored by IBM.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Japan links Chinese hacker MirrorFace to dozens of cyberattacks targeting security and tech data
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan on Wednesday linked more than 200 cyberattacks over the past five years targeting the country's national security and high technology data to a Chinese hacking group, MirrorFace, detailing their tactics and calling on government agencies and businesses to reinforce preventive meas
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 6 fast ways to free up space on your Windows PC
item.description: Want to free up space on your computer? With the right tools, you can quickly eliminate gigabytes of unnecessary files and get back to work — or whatever else you use your computer for. I’ve long argued you don’t need a “PC cleaner” app. You mostly just need the tools built right into Windows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta eliminates fact-checking in latest bow to Trump
item.description: Meta chief executive officer Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday announced changes to content moderation on Facebook and Instagram long sought by conservatives. Incoming President Donald Trump said the new approach was “probably” due to threats he made against the technology mogul.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, BARBARA ORTUTAY and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal IT skills shortage: How AI can help relieve the gap and improve security
item.description: The global IT skills shortage is predicted to impact 90% of organizations by 2026, according to IDC. The post Federal IT skills shortage: How AI can help relieve the gap and improve security first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: AI detects ovarian cancer better than human doctors: Study
item.description: AI based models achieved an accuracy rate of 86% for detecting ovarian cancer, compared to 82% for human experts.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Fubo combining with Disney's Hulu + Live TV; lawsuit against Venu Sports settled
item.description: Disney's Hulu + Live TV and sports streaming service Fubo are combining in a deal that will also see the settlement of a lawsuit against the creation of Venu Sports.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cyber security agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
